title: Lightside Explorer
content:
{{Top|fa|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:Ships2.jpg]] |name=''Lightside Explorer'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc./Legends|Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc.]]''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' |line= |model=[[Scout ship/Legends|Scout ship]] |type=[[Vaya-class scout|''Vaya''-class scout ship]] |cost=*45,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] (new) *15,000 credits (used) |modifier= |sysmods= |length=30 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine=6 [[Ion engine/Legends|ion engines]] |hyperdrive=Class 8 |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation=Equipped[[Tales of the Jedi 3|''Tales of the Jedi'' 3]] |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*[[Laser cannon/Legends|Laser cannon]] *[[Concussion projectile launcher]] |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=*1 [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] *2 [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] |skeleton= |passengers=4 |capacity=60 tons |cargohandling= |consumables=1 [[Standard month/Legends|month]] |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role= |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles= |affiliation=[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |navy= |fleet= |owners=*[[Andur Sunrider]] *[[Nomi Sunrider]] |captains= |crewmembers=[[A-3DO]] |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|All right boys, let's peel open this ship like a can o' ''nerf gizzards!''|Gudb|audio=NerfGizzards-TotJ.flac|[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]}} The '''''Lightside Explorer''''' was the personal [[Vaya-class scout|''Vaya''-class scout ship]] of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Andur Sunrider]] in the [[Time/Legends|years]] leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Having bought the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] from a [[Vultan]] [[Unidentified Vultan starship dealer|starship dealer]] sometime before [[3999 BBY]], Sunrider used the ship for [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] business across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. In 3999 BBY, he and [[Sunrider family|his family]], along with their [[Droid/Legends|droid]], [[A-3DO]], went on a journey to deliver a shipment of valuable [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]] to the reclusive [[Tchuukthai]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]]. However, Sunrider was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by a [[Bogga's enforcer gang|group]] of thugs who were seeking the [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]], forcing his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Nomi Sunrider]], to take possession of the ''Lightside Explorer'' and complete the delivery. Nomi eventually landed the ship on the desolate [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]], where Thon resided, and delivered the crystals. However, the thugs were still in pursuit and attacked the ''Lightside Explorer'' in the mistaken belief that the crystals were still aboard. When they came up empty-handed, they launched a strike on [[Ambria Jedi Enclave|Thon's compound]], but the Jedi Master [[Skirmish on Ambria|fought them off]]. ==Description== {{Quote|I was right about the hyperdrive! It was a burned-out ion exciter! Once it's replaced, the ''Lightside Explorer'' will be ready to depart!''"
"''Thanks, Threedee. There's a spare in the tool crib.|A-3DO and Andur Sunrider|audio=ThreeDeeandAndur-TotJ.flac|}} A [[gray]]-colored, triangular vessel, the ''Lightside Explorer'' was a [[Vaya-class scout|''Vaya''-class scout ship]] manufactured by [[Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc./Legends|Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc.]] Although not built for prolonged [[Space warfare/Legends|combat engagement]], the ''Lightside Explorer'' boasted both a [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] and a [[concussion projectile launcher]], and made an excellent exploratory and [[Scout ship/Legends|scout vessel]]. The ''Lightside Explorer'' was not modified or altered in any way by its owner, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Andur Sunrider]], who lacked the finer skills of [[Starship/Legends|starship]] modification. The ship was propelled by six [[Ion engine/Legends|ion engines]] and was thirty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long. The ''Lightside Explorer'' needed only one [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] but had room for two [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]], along with four passengers. It also had the capacity to carry sixty tons of cargo and could store enough consumables to last the crew one [[Standard month/Legends|standard month]]. The [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] of the ship was located at the very front, with controls and a [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] placed directly before the pilot and co-pilot's chairs. The ''Lightside Explorer'' contained a [[tool crib]], where devices such as spare [[ion exciter]]s were kept. This was important to the ''Lightside Explorer'', as the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] was susceptible to having its ion exciter burn out in situations involving high magnetic energy. ==History== ===Death of Andur Sunrider=== {{Quote|Go to Master Thon… take my ''gift'' to him. He will show you the direction your life must take.|Andur Sunrider's Force ghost, to Nomi Sunrider|}} [[File:ExplorerInFlight.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The ''Lightside Explorer'' flies toward the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal.]] The ''Lightside Explorer'' was manufactured by Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc. sometime before [[3999 BBY]] and eventually fell into the hands of a [[Vultan]] [[Unidentified Vultan starship dealer|starship dealer]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nak Shimor]]. At some point, the Vultan sold the ''Lightside Explorer'' to Andur Sunrider, a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knight. Sunrider, who had a [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Nomi Sunrider|Nomi]], and an infant [[Gender/Legends|daughter]], [[Vima Sunrider|Vima]], was often forced to leave them when he was called away for Jedi business. However, he always returned in the ''Lightside Explorer'' when his work was done.[[Tales of the Jedi 4|''Tales of the Jedi'' 4]] In 3999 BBY, Sunrider, along with Nomi, Vima, and their personal [[3DO protocol/service droid|protocol droid]], [[A-3DO]], set out on a long journey in the ''Lightside Explorer'' to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] frontier to visit with the [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Watchman]] of the [[Ambria system/Legends|Stenness system]], the [[Tchuukthai]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]]. Sunrider wanted to meet with Thon to deliver to him a shipment of [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]], valuable gems that could help make very powerful [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]]. While making the trip, the ''Lightside Explorer'' traveled through a magnetic storm, damaging the hyperdrive's ion exciter and forcing the Sunriders to land on an outcropping on a [[Unidentified rocky planet (Andur Sunrider)|rocky planet]] to assess the damage. A-3DO quickly diagnosed the problem and replaced the damaged ion exciter with a spare. With the hyperdrive operational, the ''Lightside Explorer'' sped away from the planet, soon reaching a nearby [[Hyperspace beacon/Legends|hyperspace beacon]] and leaping into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. The ''Lightside Explorer'' came out of hyperspace at the [[Stenness Hyperspace Terminal]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]], located near the Stenness [[Star system/Legends|system]]. Andur Sunrider parked the ''Lightside Explorer'' in one of the Terminal's [[Hangar/Legends|docking bays]], hoping to take a break and get some food while the ship went through customs, although the arrival of the ''Lightside Explorer'' had attracted some unwanted attention. Local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], leader of a [[Bogga's enforcer gang|gang]] of enforcers and thugs, had discovered the existence of the Adegan [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] and dispatched three of his henchmen to steal them. The thugs accosted Sunrider, and after a brief struggle Sunrider was [[Death/Legends|killed]]. Sunrider's [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] then appeared to Nomi, and the deceased Jedi guided his widow to take up his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and fight off the enforcers. In his final message to his wife, the ghost of Andur Sunrider asked her to take the controls of the ''Lightside Explorer'' and deliver the crystals to Thon, who, Sunrider promised, would show Nomi the direction her life must take. ===Ambria=== {{Quote|Threedee…if something happens… if we don't come back…take the ship…return to our homeworld…tell them what happened.|Nomi Sunrider, to A-3DO|audio=IfWeDontComeBack-TotJ.flac|}} [[File:ExplorerOnAmbria.jpg|thumb|right|325px|The ''Lightside Explorer'' lands on Ambria]] Nomi Sunrider then left the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal in the ''Lightside Explorer'', using only her [[Force-sensitive/Legends|abilities]] with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to guide her to Thon. Although the ship's [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]], communicating through A-3DO, was nervous and wanted to know their exact destination, Sunrider responded that she trusted in the Force and that there would be no need to worry. Eventually, Sunrider brought the ''Lightside Explorer'' out of hyperspace at the desolate [[Stenness Node]] planet of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]]. Landing the ship on Ambria's rocky surface, Sunrider commented that she was totally sure that this planet would be where she would find Thon. She took baby Vima along with her on her trek through the desert, while A-3DO stayed behind in the ''Lightside Explorer'', as his mechanical joints would seize up if he ventured into the dry wastes of Ambria. While Sunrider was out on her search for Thon, A-3DO and the ''Lightside Explorer'' received some unexpected visitors: a team of Bogga's men, led by [[Gudb]], broke into the ship and ransacked it, looking for the Adegan crystals. Armed with [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], the thugs shot one of A-3DO's arms off in the commotion, although they failed to find the crystals, which were on Sunrider's person. The enforcers left the ''Lightside Explorer'' behind and journeyed farther into the wastes, eventually coming across [[Ambria Jedi Enclave|Thon's compound]], where Sunrider had gone with the crystals. However, Thon [[Skirmish on Ambria|fought them off]] and forced them to flee. After the attack on the ''Lightside Explorer'', the ship was visited by one of Thon's [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi trainees]], the Vultan [[Oss Wilum]]. Wilum removed the damaged A-3DO and brought him to Thon's compound, where he repaired the [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. The ''Lightside Explorer'' was then moved closer to Thon's compound, where Sunrider was undergoing [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]]. One day, Nomi and Vima went out for training with Thon; A-3DO opted to stay behind and spent his time repairing the ''Lightside Explorer''{{'s}} hatchway, which had been damaged in the attack by Bogga's enforcers. Before she left, Sunrider told A-3DO that he should take the ''Lightside Explorer'' and go to Andur's former Jedi Master, [[Chamma]], and tell him everything that had happened, should they fail to return. Eventually, Bogga's men returned for another attempt at stealing the Adegan crystals. Sunrider and Thon, who had just returned from their excursion, came back to their compound to find it bombed and surrounded by the thugs. Although A-3DO had completely repaired the ''Lightside Explorer''{{'s}} hatch, Sunrider and the droid could not reach the ship, as the enforcers were in their way. Meanwhile, Thon refused to fight back, and he was taken [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] by the thugs; instead, Sunrider used the [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] to turn the invaders against each other. While the thugs fought, Sunrider [[Skirmish on Ambria|attacked]] with a lightsaber, killing many of them. She and Thon then teamed up to take on the rest, and the ones who were not killed in the ensuing battle fled.[[Tales of the Jedi 5|''Tales of the Jedi'' 5]] ==Commanders and crew== The [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and owner of the ''Lightside Explorer'' was Jedi Knight Andur Sunrider, who bought the ship from a Vultan starship dealer some time before 3999 BBY. Sunrider also piloted the ship, with his personal [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol/service droid]] A-3DO often serving as his co-pilot. Sunrider often took risks while flying the ''Lightside Explorer'', including flying it through a magnetic storm, much to the chagrin of his droid companion. After the death of Andur, his wife, Nomi, took over as the pilot and owner of the ship. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Lightside Explorer'' first appears in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Saga of Nomi Sunrider|''Tales of the Jedi: The Saga of Nomi Sunrider'']], the second story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and released over late [[1993]]–early [[1994]]. The ship appears in all three of the arc's issues, as the vehicle of the story's protagonist, Nomi Sunrider. In these appearances the ''Lighside Explorer'' is illustrated by [[Janine Johnston]] and later [[David Roach]]. The ship also appears in the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio adaptation]], which includes the events of ''The Saga of Nomi Sunrider''. Further information on the ship is given in the ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' reference book, released in [[1996]]. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] *[[Tales of the Jedi 3|''Tales of the Jedi'' 3]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi 4|''Tales of the Jedi'' 4]] *[[Tales of the Jedi 5|''Tales of the Jedi'' 5]] ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|27|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 2}} *''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Lightside Explorer }} [[Category:Starships of the Jedi Order]] [[Category:Vaya-class scouts]]
